category_title: Students with emotional disturbance and autism exhibiting significant behavioral challenges
minimal_context: The student is working in a cooperative learning group with peers to complete a shared assignment.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please work with your group to finish the science poster.

STUDENT: I'm not doing it. He keeps staring at me and trying to mess up my part.

TUTOR: Just focus on your own section and keep working.

STUDENT: (Stands up from the group table) You're doing it on purpose! Stop looking at me!

TUTOR: You need to sit back down and finish the group assignment.

STUDENT: I'm going to break your face! (Sweeps the poster and markers off the table onto the floor)

options.A: Award points at the end of the session during brief private meetings in an instructive and encouraging manner.
options.B: Use technology during cooperative group assignments to create spaces of inclusion and acknowledgement for the student.
options.C: Purposefully select science poster materials created by and/or featuring people of color for the group assignments.
options.D: Use levels of engagement during cooperative learning groups as a quick screening assessment for the student.
